dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses Bollinger Bands (upper/lower bands) for entry and exit. 'Upper band is broken' maps to close crossing above bb_upper; 'Lower band is crossed' maps to close crossing below bb_lower. System is always in market with reversal logic: exiting long when lower band crosses (triggering short entry) and vice versa. Volatility-dependent band width is an intrinsic property of Bollinger Bands calculation and does not require separate conditions.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses Bollinger Bands (upper/lower bands) for entry and exit. 'Upper band is broken' maps to close crossing above bb_upper; 'Lower band is crossed' maps to close crossing below bb_lower. System is always in market with reversal logic: exiting long when lower band crosses (triggering short entry) and vice versa. Volatility-dependent band width is an intrinsic property of Bollinger Bands calculation and does not require separate conditions.

source_rule_text: Take long positions when the upper band is broken and short positions when the lower band is crossed; the system is always in the market.
